_doc: Goals are grouped into CATEGORY BUCKETS under `goals` so like kinds sit together:
- `milestones`     : execution-derived scopes from a Xenia trace (the accurate kind).
Created by `work goal import-trace <name>`; defined by `include_tus`.
- `pattern_slices` : hand-authored glob scopes for working a whole subsystem (approximate;
NOT runnable milestones). Defined by `include`/`exclude` globs.
Goal NAMES are unique across buckets. `active_goal` names one of them (or null = whole-program).
A goal's scope = `include_tus` UNION (TUs matching `include` globs, minus `exclude` globs),
where globs (`*` = any chars) match a TU id AND the function names it contains. Tune any scope
with `work goal show <name>` (boundary report = what it will trap-stub). See
references/GOAL_SCOPING.md for the full guide incl. how to capture a Xenia trace.

boot-trace.description: MILESTONE (execution-derived): boot to the Main Menu, no input. Includes the gameplay/world systems the engine preloads in the background before the menu shows (junkyard, racecars, traffic init) - that code really runs, so it is in scope.

boot-to-main-menu.description: PATTERN SLICE (hand-authored globs, approximate): front-end/GUI/director subsystems. For the real boot-to-menu milestone use the trace-derived `boot-trace` goal instead.

loading-screen.description: PATTERN SLICE (hand-authored globs, approximate): loading/boot-screen GUI states only — NOT a runnable milestone (its render/init callees are out of scope). Prefer a trace goal for milestones.
